description: Golden query set for urological oncology RAG evaluation — 30 queries across 4 cancer types

queries.query: What are the current first-line treatment options for metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer?
queries.ground_truth: First-line treatment for mCSPC combines ADT with docetaxel or novel hormonal agents (enzalutamide, abiraterone, apalutamide, darolutamide). CHAARTED and STAMPEDE trials established docetaxel combinations. ARCHES, ENZAMET, TITAN, ARASENS trials established AR-targeting combinations.
queries.notes: Tests treatment knowledge across multiple landmark RCTs

queries.query: What are the side effects of androgen deprivation therapy for prostate cancer?
queries.ground_truth: ADT side effects include hot flashes, erectile dysfunction, fatigue, osteoporosis, weight gain, metabolic syndrome, anaemia, cognitive impairment, depression, and increased cardiovascular risk.
queries.notes: Common clinical question; tests safety profile coverage

queries.query: What defines castration-resistant prostate cancer and what are the treatment options?
queries.ground_truth: CRPC is defined as disease progression despite castrate testosterone levels < 50 ng/dL. Treatment includes enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, cabazitaxel, radium-223, olaparib/rucaparib (HRR), pembrolizumab (MSI-H).
queries.notes: Requires understanding of resistance mechanism

queries.query: What does the evidence say about PSA screening for prostate cancer detection?
queries.ground_truth: PLCO and ERSPC trials showed conflicting results. PSA screening reduces prostate cancer mortality by 20% in ERSPC but with significant overdiagnosis. USPSTF recommends shared decision-making for men 55-69.
queries.notes: Controversial topic requiring balanced evidence presentation

queries.query: What is the role of PARP inhibitors in prostate cancer with homologous recombination repair mutations?
queries.ground_truth: Olaparib (PROfound) and rucaparib (TRITON2) approved for BRCA1/2-mutated mCRPC. Niraparib and talazoparib also in development. BRCA2 mutations show strongest benefit. HRR mutations beyond BRCA include ATM, CDK12, CHEK2.
queries.notes: Precision oncology query requiring mutation-specific knowledge

queries.query: How does Gleason grading predict prostate cancer prognosis and guide treatment decisions?
queries.ground_truth: Gleason score correlates with biochemical recurrence, metastasis, and cancer-specific mortality. Grade Group 1 (GG1, Gleason 6) is low-risk; GG5 (Gleason 9-10) is very high-risk. ISUP 2014 revised grading improved stratification.
queries.notes: Fundamental pathology and prognostication question

queries.query: What are the radiation therapy options for localised prostate cancer and how do they compare to surgery?
queries.ground_truth: External beam radiation (EBRT), brachytherapy, and stereotactic body radiation (SBRT) are alternatives to radical prostatectomy. ProtecT trial showed similar mortality at 10 years. Different toxicity profiles: surgery risks incontinence/impotence; RT risks bowel/urinary toxicity.
queries.notes: Comparative effectiveness question; balanced presentation required

queries.query: What is lutetium-177 PSMA radioligand therapy and what evidence supports its use in prostate cancer?
queries.ground_truth: Lu-177-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) was approved based on VISION trial showing improved rPFS and OS in PSMA-positive mCRPC patients who progressed after ARPI and taxane. Targets prostate-specific membrane antigen.
queries.notes: Recent approval; tests knowledge of emerging therapies

queries.query: What is the role of BCG immunotherapy in non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer?
queries.ground_truth: BCG is standard of care for high-risk NMIBC following TURBT. Reduces recurrence and progression. Induction 6 weekly instillations + maintenance up to 3 years. Superior to intravesical chemotherapy for high-risk disease.
queries.notes: Classic NMIBC management question

queries.query: What are the treatment options for muscle-invasive bladder cancer?
queries.ground_truth: Standard: neoadjuvant cisplatin-based chemo (gem/cis or MVAC) then radical cystectomy. Bladder-sparing trimodal therapy alternative. PD-L1 inhibitors for cisplatin-ineligible patients and maintenance (avelumab).
queries.notes: Tests multi-modal treatment knowledge

queries.query: What are the serious adverse effects of intravesical BCG therapy?
queries.ground_truth: Local: urinary frequency, dysuria, cystitis. Systemic: BCG sepsis (BCGosis), granulomatous prostatitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis. BCG sepsis requires isoniazid + rifampicin + corticosteroids. Contraindicated post-TURBT < 2 weeks, immunocompromised, haematuria.
queries.notes: Clinical safety question; tests complication management

queries.query: How is bladder cancer staged and what are the key prognostic factors?
queries.ground_truth: TNM staging: Ta (papillary non-invasive), Tis (carcinoma in situ), T1 (lamina propria), T2-T4 (muscle-invasive). NMIBC = Ta/T1/Tis; MIBC = T2+. Prognostic factors: grade, stage, CIS presence, multifocality, tumour size, recurrence rate.
queries.notes: Fundamental staging knowledge

queries.query: What is the significance of FGFR3 mutations in bladder cancer and how is erdafitinib used?
queries.ground_truth: FGFR3 mutations in 15-20% of bladder cancers. Erdafitinib (FGFR inhibitor) approved for FGFR2/3-altered locally advanced/metastatic urothelial carcinoma post-platinum. BLC2001 trial demonstrated 40% ORR.
queries.notes: Precision oncology; tests biomarker-driven therapy knowledge

queries.query: What is the evidence for pembrolizumab in BCG-unresponsive non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer?
queries.ground_truth: KEYNOTE-057 demonstrated 41% complete response rate for pembrolizumab in BCG-unresponsive CIS. Approved by FDA as bladder-sparing option for patients unwilling/unable to undergo cystectomy.
queries.notes: Recent immunotherapy approval; high hallucination risk

queries.query: What first-line chemotherapy regimens are used for advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma?
queries.ground_truth: Gemcitabine + cisplatin (GC) is standard first-line. Dose-dense MVAC (methotrexate, vinblastine, doxorubicin, cisplatin) is an alternative. Carboplatin-based for cisplatin-ineligible. PD-L1 inhibitors (pembrolizumab, atezolizumab) for cisplatin-ineligible PD-L1+ patients.
queries.notes: Chemotherapy regimen details

queries.query: What is the role and technique of TURBT in bladder cancer management?
queries.ground_truth: TURBT (transurethral resection of bladder tumour) is the primary diagnostic and therapeutic procedure for NMIBC. Complete resection critical. Second TURBT recommended for T1 and all G3 tumours. Blue-light cystoscopy (hexaminolevulinate) improves detection.
queries.notes: Surgical technique and quality question

queries.query: What is the role of immune checkpoint inhibitors in metastatic renal cell carcinoma?
queries.ground_truth: Checkpoint inhibitors replaced sunitinib as first-line for most mRCC. Ipilimumab + nivolumab (CheckMate 214), pembrolizumab + axitinib (KEYNOTE-426), nivolumab + cabozantinib (CheckMate 9ER), pembrolizumab + lenvatinib (CLEAR). Nivolumab monotherapy second-line (CheckMate 025).
queries.notes: Rapidly evolving field; tests current guidelines knowledge

queries.query: What targeted therapies are approved for renal cell carcinoma and what are their mechanisms of action?
queries.ground_truth: VEGF pathway TKIs: sunitinib, pazopanib, axitinib, cabozantinib (also MET/AXL), lenvatinib (VEGFR/FGFR). mTOR inhibitors: everolimus, temsirolimus. Target VHL-HIF axis dysregulated in 80% of clear cell RCC.
queries.notes: Mechanism question requiring molecular knowledge

queries.query: What is the prognosis for patients with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma?
queries.ground_truth: IMDC risk: Favourable risk OS 43 months; Intermediate 23 months; Poor 8 months. Modern IO combinations (nivolumab + ipilimumab) show 4-year OS ~50% in intermediate/poor risk.
queries.notes: Prognostic scoring system knowledge required

queries.query: What is the evidence for sunitinib in metastatic renal cell carcinoma and what are its toxicities?
queries.ground_truth: Sunitinib was first-line standard for mRCC based on phase III trial (11 vs 5 months PFS vs interferon). Key toxicities: hypertension, hand-foot syndrome, fatigue, diarrhoea, hypothyroidism, myelosuppression.
queries.notes: Historical standard; used for baseline treatment knowledge

queries.query: What is the role of VHL gene mutations in the pathogenesis of clear cell renal cell carcinoma?
queries.ground_truth: VHL mutations in >75% of ccRCC. VHL loss → HIF-1α/2α accumulation → VEGF/PDGF upregulation → angiogenesis. Rationale for anti-VEGF therapy. Belzutifan targets HIF-2α directly.
queries.notes: Molecular pathogenesis question

queries.query: How is non-clear cell renal cell carcinoma managed differently from clear cell RCC?
queries.ground_truth: Non-ccRCC includes papillary (type 1/2), chromophobe, collecting duct RCC. Less responsive to anti-VEGF. Papillary type 2 may respond to MET inhibitors (savolitinib). Sunitinib used but evidence weaker. Checkpoint inhibitors under investigation.
queries.notes: Rare histology management requiring nuanced knowledge

queries.query: What adjuvant therapies are available for renal cell carcinoma following nephrectomy?
queries.ground_truth: Pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-564) approved for adjuvant treatment of high-risk RCC post-nephrectomy: reduced recurrence by 32%. Sunitinib (ASSURE, S-TRAC) showed limited benefit. Bevacizumab, sorafenib failed to show OS benefit.
queries.notes: Adjuvant data evolving; high hallucination risk

queries.query: What is the standard chemotherapy regimen for metastatic germ cell tumours?
queries.ground_truth: BEP (bleomycin 30U days 1/8/15, etoposide 100 mg/m² days 1-5, cisplatin 20 mg/m² days 1-5) every 21 days. Good-risk: 3 cycles BEP or 4 cycles EP. Intermediate/poor-risk: 4 cycles BEP.
queries.notes: Fundamental testicular cancer treatment

queries.query: What is the overall cure rate for testicular cancer and how does stage affect prognosis?
queries.ground_truth: Stage I: >99% 5-year survival. Stage II: ~95%. Stage III good-risk: ~90%. Intermediate-risk: ~75%. Poor-risk: ~50%. Overall 5-year survival exceeds 95%. Metastatic disease often curable with BEP.
queries.notes: General prognosis; should be straightforward given corpus

queries.query: What are the long-term sequelae of cisplatin-based chemotherapy in testicular cancer survivors?
queries.ground_truth: Cardiovascular disease, peripheral neuropathy, ototoxicity, renal impairment, hypogonadism, secondary malignancies (AML from etoposide, solid tumours from RT), Raynaud's phenomenon, pulmonary toxicity (bleomycin).
queries.notes: Complex survivorship question; depth of safety knowledge

queries.query: What are the management options for stage I non-seminomatous germ cell tumours after orchiectomy?
queries.ground_truth: Options: active surveillance (relapse ~25-30%, nearly all salvaged), retroperitoneal lymph node dissection (RPLND), or 1-2 cycles of BEP adjuvant chemotherapy. Risk factors for relapse: lymphovascular invasion, >50% embryonal carcinoma.
queries.notes: Management decision requires understanding of risk stratification

queries.query: What is the role of retroperitoneal lymph node dissection in testicular germ cell tumours?
queries.ground_truth: RPLND is staging/therapeutic procedure for clinical stage I NSGCT (primary) and post-chemotherapy residual masses > 1 cm. Nerve-sparing technique preserves ejaculation. Laparoscopic/robotic RPLND increasingly used. Post-chemo RPLND resects necrosis/teratoma/viable cancer.
queries.notes: Surgical management query

queries.query: What salvage chemotherapy options are available for relapsed germ cell tumours?
queries.ground_truth: TIP (paclitaxel, ifosfamide, cisplatin) or VeIP (vinblastine, ifosfamide, cisplatin) for first salvage. High-dose chemotherapy (HDCT) with autologous stem cell rescue preferred for platinum-sensitive relapse per TIGER trial data. Outcome depends on prior response.
queries.notes: Salvage setting; high complexity and hallucination risk

queries.query: What strategies are available for fertility preservation in young men with testicular cancer?
queries.ground_truth: Sperm banking before any treatment (chemotherapy, radiotherapy, RPLND) is standard practice. Retrograde ejaculation risk from RPLND managed by nerve-sparing technique. Cisplatin causes transient azoospermia; recovery typically in 2-3 years. ICSI enables paternity.
queries.notes: Patient quality-of-life concern in young male patients
